name: clamav-freshclam
title: clamav-freshclam
summary: anti-virus utility for Unix - virus database update utility
description:
anti-virus utility for Unix - virus database update utility
Clam AntiVirus is an anti-virus toolkit for Unix. The main purpose of
this software is the integration with mail servers (attachment
scanning). The package provides a flexible and scalable
multi-threaded daemon in the clamav-daemon package, a command-line
scanner in the clamav package, and a tool for automatic updating via
the Internet in the clamav-freshclam package. The programs are based
on libclamav, which can be used by other software.
.
This package contains the freshclam program and scripts to automate virus
database updating. It relies on an Internet connection, but can be
run in a variety of ways to compensate for intermittent connections.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use freshclam because anti-virus utility for Unix - virus database update utility.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use freshclam as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `freshclam [options]` as documented upstream for freshclam.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `freshclam` as documented upstream for freshclam.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `freshclam --datadir=.` as documented upstream for freshclam.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `freshclam -d -c 2` as documented upstream for freshclam.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair freshclam with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `freshclam --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
